◐ lead-gua · assign_scenarios: shopsystem-bc-launcher — propagate host credentials (~/.claude, ~/.config/gh, ~/.gitconfig) into launched BC containers   [● P1 · IN_PROGRESS]
Owner: David Stenglein · Assignee: David Stenglein · Type: feature
Created: 2026-05-22 · Started: 2026-05-22 · Updated: 2026-05-22

DESCRIPTION
bc-launcher's controller.launch() currently mounts only the BC's own workspace and an optional SHOPMSG socket (controller.py:139-149). Launched BCs cannot authenticate to GitHub (no ~/.config/gh), cannot use claude-code (no ~/.claude), and cannot identify themselves to git (no ~/.gitconfig). The canonical credential-mount pattern is in evidence at /workspaces/shopsystem-product/.devcontainer/devcontainer.json (lines 4-9). Verified: grep -rn 'credential|.claude|.config/gh|.gitconfig' repos/shopsystem-bc-launcher/features/ returns no matches; no scenario covers credential propagation today. Discriminator: capability genuinely absent → assign_scenarios. SEQUENCING NOTE: scenarios for this work need PO authorship before dispatch (this bead is not yet ready to send).

NOTES
2026-05-22: BC emitted clarify on the on-wire work_id shopsystem-templates-918 asking how launcher should behave when host $HOME/.claude/.claude.json is absent (capability today: cp exec_run runs unconditionally, return code ignored — silent skip). PO answered (b) warn-to-stderr-and-proceed. Architect dispatched clarify-resolution as request_bugfix on a fresh work_id lead-bxl carrying scenario 08 (warns-and-proceeds). Original 918 dispatch remains in flight; this bugfix is additive, not a supersession. Per lead-d6o protocol-gap pattern (no native lead→BC clarify-response vehicle in shop-msg send).

1	"""
2	bc-container CLI entry point.
3	
4	Subcommands: launch, attach, inject, monitor, stop, status, list, manifest
5	"""
6	from __future__ import annotations
7	
8	import argparse
9	import sys
10	from pathlib import Path
11	
12	from bc_launcher.controller import BcContainerController
13	from bc_launcher.driver import RealDockerDriver
14	from bc_launcher.manifest import ManifestController, RealGitDriver, RealGitHubDriver
15	
16	
17	def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
18	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
19	        prog="bc-container",
20	        description="Manage BC Docker containers for the shopsystem framework.",
21	    )
22	    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="subcommand", required=True)
23	
24	    # launch
25	    p_launch = sub.add_parser("launch", help="Start a BC container")
26	    p_launch.add_argument("bc_name", help="BC name (e.g. shopsystem-messaging)")
27	    p_launch.add_argument("--repo-url", help="Git repo URL to clone inside the container")
28	    p_launch.add_argument("--shopmsg-dsn", help="SHOPMSG_DSN value for the container")
29	    p_launch.add_argument("--network", help="Docker network to attach")
30	    p_launch.add_argument("--startup-prompt", help="Text to inject into tmux after start")
31	
32	    # attach
33	    p_attach = sub.add_parser("attach", help="Attach to the BC container tmux session")
34	    p_attach.add_argument("bc_name", help="BC name")
35	
36	    # inject
37	    p_inject = sub.add_parser("inject", help="Send text to the BC container tmux session")
38	    p_inject.add_argument("bc_name", help="BC name")
39	    p_inject.add_argument("prompt", help="Text to send")
40	
41	    # monitor
42	    p_monitor = sub.add_parser("monitor", help="Stream BC container tmux output")
43	    p_monitor.add_argument("bc_name", help="BC name")
44	
45	    # stop
46	    p_stop = sub.add_parser("stop", help="Stop and remove the BC container")
47	    p_stop.add_argument("bc_name", help="BC name")
48	
49	    # status
50	    p_status = sub.add_parser("status", help="Report BC container state")
51	    p_status.add_argument("bc_name", help="BC name")
52	
53	    # list
54	    sub.add_parser("list", help="List all known BC containers")
55	
56	    # manifest
57	    p_manifest = sub.add_parser("manifest", help="Manage the BC manifest file")
58	    manifest_sub = p_manifest.add_subparsers(dest="manifest_subcommand", required=True)
59	
60	    # manifest validate
61	    p_mv = manifest_sub.add_parser("validate", help="Validate the BC manifest")
62	    p_mv.add_argument(
63	        "--manifest", default="bc-manifest.yaml",
64	        help="Path to the manifest file (default: bc-manifest.yaml)",
65	    )
66	    p_mv.add_argument(
67	        "--repos-dir", default=None,
68	        help="Path to the repos directory for consistency checks",
69	    )
70	
71	    # manifest list
72	    p_ml = manifest_sub.add_parser("list", help="List BCs declared in the manifest")
73	    p_ml.add_argument(
74	        "--manifest", default="bc-manifest.yaml",
75	        help="Path to the manifest file (default: bc-manifest.yaml)",
76	    )
77	
78	    # manifest sync
79	    p_ms = manifest_sub.add_parser("sync", help="Sync repos directory from the manifest")
80	    p_ms.add_argument(
81	        "--manifest", default="bc-manifest.yaml",
82	        help="Path to the manifest file (default: bc-manifest.yaml)",
83	    )
84	    p_ms.add_argument(
85	        "--repos-dir", default="repos",
86	        help="Path to the repos directory (default: repos)",
87	    )
88	
89	    return parser
90	
91	
92	def _run_manifest(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
93	    """Handle 'bc-container manifest <subcommand>'."""
94	    mc = ManifestController(
95	        github_driver=RealGitHubDriver(),
96	        git_driver=RealGitDriver(),
97	    )
98	    manifest_path = Path(args.manifest)
99	
100	    if args.manifest_subcommand == "validate":
101	        repos_dir = Path(args.repos_dir) if args.repos_dir else None
102	        result = mc.validate(manifest_path, repos_dir=repos_dir)
103	        for msg in result.messages:
104	            sys.stdout.write(msg + "\n")
105	        return 0 if result.ok else 1
106	
107	    elif args.manifest_subcommand == "list":
108	        exit_code, output = mc.list_bcs(manifest_path)
109	        sys.stdout.write(output)
110	        return exit_code
111	
112	    elif args.manifest_subcommand == "sync":
113	        repos_dir = Path(args.repos_dir)
114	        exit_code, output = mc.sync(manifest_path, repos_dir)
115	        sys.stdout.write(output)
116	        return exit_code
117	
118	    return 0
119	
120	
121	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
122	    parser = build_parser()
123	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
124	
125	    if args.subcommand == "manifest":
126	        return _run_manifest(args)
127	
128	    controller = BcContainerController(RealDockerDriver())
129	
130	    if args.subcommand == "launch":
131	        result = controller.launch(
132	            bc_name=args.bc_name,
133	            repo_url=getattr(args, "repo_url", None),
134	            shopmsg_dsn=getattr(args, "shopmsg_dsn", None),
135	            startup_prompt=getattr(args, "startup_prompt", None),
136	            network=getattr(args, "network", None),
137	        )
138	        sys.stdout.write(result.stdout)
139	        if result.stderr:
140	            sys.stderr.write(result.stderr)
141	        return result.exit_code
142	
143	    elif args.subcommand == "attach":
144	        # attach replaces the process — no return value
145	        controller.attach(args.bc_name)
146	        return 0  # unreachable in production
147	
148	    elif args.subcommand == "inject":
149	        result = controller.inject(args.bc_name, args.prompt)
150	        sys.stdout.write(result.stdout)
151	        if result.stderr:
152	            sys.stderr.write(result.stderr)
153	        return result.exit_code
154	
155	    elif args.subcommand == "monitor":
156	        result = controller.monitor(args.bc_name)
157	        sys.stdout.write(result.stdout)
158	        if result.stderr:
159	            sys.stderr.write(result.stderr)
160	        return result.exit_code
161	
162	    elif args.subcommand == "stop":
163	        result = controller.stop(args.bc_name)
164	        sys.stdout.write(result.stdout)
165	        if result.stderr:
166	            sys.stderr.write(result.stderr)
167	        return result.exit_code
168	
169	    elif args.subcommand == "status":
170	        result = controller.status(args.bc_name)
171	        sys.stdout.write(result.stdout)
172	        if result.stderr:
173	            sys.stderr.write(result.stderr)
174	        return result.exit_code
175	
176	    elif args.subcommand == "list":
177	        result = controller.list_containers()
178	        sys.stdout.write(result.stdout)
179	        if result.stderr:
180	            sys.stderr.write(result.stderr)
181	        return result.exit_code
182	
183	    return 0
184	
185	
186	if __name__ == "__main__":
187	    sys.exit(main())
188
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./repos/shopsystem-bc-launcher/src/bc_launcher/controller.py`

```text
1	"""
2	Business logic for bc-container subcommands.
3	
4	All Docker interaction goes through the DockerDriver interface, making this
5	layer fully testable without a live Docker daemon.
6	"""
7	from __future__ import annotations
8	
9	import os
10	import re
11	import sys
12	from dataclasses import dataclass
13	from pathlib import Path
14	
15	from bc_launcher.driver import ContainerMount, DockerDriver
16	
17	
18	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
19	# Constants / defaults
20	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
21	
22	CONTAINER_WORKSPACE = "/workspace"
23	AGENT_TMUX_SESSION = "agent"
24	BC_IMAGE = "ghcr.io/shopsystem/bc-base:latest"
25	SHOPMSG_DSN_ENV = "SHOPMSG_DSN"
26	
27	
28	def _container_name(bc_name: str) -> str:
29	    return f"bc-{bc_name}"
30	
31	
32	def _slugify(text: str) -> str:
33	    """Lowercase and replace runs of spaces with hyphens."""
34	    return re.sub(r"\s+", "-", text.strip().lower())
35	
36	
37	def _read_product_from_manifest(manifest_path: Path) -> str | None:
38	    """Read the 'product' field from a bc-manifest.yaml file.
39	
40	    Returns None if the file does not exist or has no 'product' key.
41	    Raises yaml.YAMLError on parse failure.
42	    """
43	    import yaml
44	    if not manifest_path.exists():
45	        return None
46	    data = yaml.safe_load(manifest_path.read_text())
47	    if isinstance(data, dict):
48	        return data.get("product")
49	    return None
50	
51	
52	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
53	# Result type returned by commands that produce output
54	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
55	
56	@dataclass
57	class CommandResult:
58	    exit_code: int
59	    stdout: str
60	    stderr: str = ""
61	
62	
63	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
64	# Subcommand implementations
65	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
66	
67	class BcContainerController:
68	    """
69	    Pure-Python controller for bc-container operations.
70	
71	    Accepts a DockerDriver at construction time so tests can inject fakes.
72	    """
73	
74	    def __init__(self, driver: DockerDriver) -> None:
75	        self._driver = driver
76	
77	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
78	    # launch
79	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
80	
81	    def launch(
82	        self,
83	        bc_name: str,
84	        repo_url: str | None = None,
85	        shopmsg_dsn: str | None = None,
86	        startup_prompt: str | None = None,
87	        network: str | None = None,
88	        manifest_path: Path | None = None,
89	        credential_home: Path | None = None,
90	    ) -> CommandResult:
91	        """
92	        Start a Docker container for the named BC.
93	
94	        Idempotent: if the container is already running, report and exit 0.
95	
96	        Network resolution (in priority order):
97	        1. If ``network`` is provided explicitly, use it as-is (no auto-create).
98	        2. Otherwise, read ``product:`` from bc-manifest.yaml (at ``manifest_path``
99	           or ``Path("bc-manifest.yaml")`` in CWD), slugify it, and use that as the
100	           network name.  If the network does not yet exist, create it first.
101	        3. If neither source is available, return a non-zero error.
102	
103	        Credential propagation:
104	        The following host paths are bind-mounted into the container so that
105	        Claude and GitHub credentials are available to the agent:
106	          - $HOME/.claude       → /home/vscode/.claude        (read-write)
107	          - $HOME/.config/gh    → /home/vscode/.config/gh     (read-write)
108	          - $HOME/.gitconfig    → /tmp/host-gitconfig          (read-only)
109	        After container start, the controller copies:
110	          - /tmp/host-gitconfig → /home/vscode/.gitconfig
111	          - /home/vscode/.claude/.claude.json → /home/vscode/.claude.json
112	        All three host paths must exist; if any is missing the launch fails fast.
113	        ``credential_home`` overrides the home directory used for these paths
114	        (useful in tests).
115	        """
116	        container = _container_name(bc_name)
117	
118	        if self._driver.is_running(container):
119	            return CommandResult(
120	                exit_code=0,
121	                stdout=f"{container} is already running\n",
122	            )
123	
124	        # --- Network resolution ---
125	        resolved_network: str | None = network
126	        auto_create_network = False
127	
128	        if resolved_network is None:
129	            # Try to derive from manifest
130	            effective_manifest = manifest_path or Path("bc-manifest.yaml")
131	            product = _read_product_from_manifest(effective_manifest)
132	            if product:
133	                resolved_network = _slugify(product)
134	                auto_create_network = True
135	            else:
136	                return CommandResult(
137	                    exit_code=1,
138	                    stdout="",
139	                    stderr="no network: bc-manifest.yaml not found and --network not provided\n",
140	                )
141	
142	        # Create the derived network if it does not yet exist (only for auto-derived, not explicit)
143	        if auto_create_network and not self._driver.network_exists(resolved_network):
144	            self._driver.network_create(resolved_network)
145	
146	        # --- Credential path resolution ---
147	        home = credential_home if credential_home is not None else Path.home()
148	        claude_dir = home / ".claude"
149	        gh_config_dir = home / ".config" / "gh"
150	        gitconfig_file = home / ".gitconfig"
151	
152	        # Fail fast if any default credential source is missing
153	        for host_path, display_name in [
154	            (claude_dir, "$HOME/.claude"),
155	            (gh_config_dir, "$HOME/.config/gh"),
156	            (gitconfig_file, "$HOME/.gitconfig"),
157	        ]:
158	            if not host_path.exists():
159	                return CommandResult(
160	                    exit_code=1,
161	                    stdout="",
162	                    stderr=f"credential source not found: {display_name}\n",
163	                )
164	
165	        # Build environment
166	        env: dict[str, str] = {}
167	        if shopmsg_dsn:
168	            env[SHOPMSG_DSN_ENV] = shopmsg_dsn
169	        elif dsn := os.environ.get(SHOPMSG_DSN_ENV):
170	            env[SHOPMSG_DSN_ENV] = dsn
171	
172	        # Mounts: credential mounts + optional SHOPMSG socket
173	        # Each entry: (type, source, dest, readonly)
174	        mounts: list[tuple[str, str, str, bool]] = []
175	
176	        # Credential bind mounts
177	        mounts.append(("bind", str(claude_dir), "/home/vscode/.claude", False))
178	        mounts.append(("bind", str(gh_config_dir), "/home/vscode/.config/gh", False))
179	        mounts.append(("bind", str(gitconfig_file), "/tmp/host-gitconfig", True))
180	
181	        # SHOPMSG_DSN may be a postgres DSN (no socket mount needed) or a
182	        # unix socket path.  If the DSN value looks like a socket file, add a
183	        # bind mount for it.
184	        dsn_value = env.get(SHOPMSG_DSN_ENV, "")
185	        if dsn_value.startswith("/") and not dsn_value.startswith("//"):
186	            # It's a host socket path — mount the containing directory
187	            socket_dir = os.path.dirname(dsn_value)
188	            mounts.append(("bind", socket_dir, socket_dir, False))
189	
190	        self._driver.run(
191	            container_name=container,
192	            image=BC_IMAGE,
193	            env=env,
194	            mounts=mounts,
195	            network=resolved_network,
196	            detach=True,
197	        )
198	
199	        out_lines: list[str] = [f"Started container {container}\n"]
200	        err_lines: list[str] = []
201	
202	        # Copy staged gitconfig into container user's home
203	        self._driver.exec_run(
204	            container,
205	            ["cp", "/tmp/host-gitconfig", "/home/vscode/.gitconfig"],
206	        )
207	        out_lines.append("Copied host gitconfig into container\n")
208	
209	        # Copy .claude.json from mounted ~/.claude into container user's home root,
210	        # but only if the host file exists.  Missing .claude.json is non-fatal:
211	        # warn to stderr and proceed (brief 007 minimum-friction posture).
212	        claude_json_file = home / ".claude" / ".claude.json"
213	        if claude_json_file.exists():
214	            self._driver.exec_run(
215	                container,
216	                ["cp", "/home/vscode/.claude/.claude.json", "/home/vscode/.claude.json"],
217	            )
218	            out_lines.append("Copied .claude.json into container home\n")
219	        else:
220	            err_lines.append(
221	                f"warning: .claude.json not found; skipping copy into container\n"
222	            )
223	
224	        # Clone repository if URL provided
225	        if repo_url:
226	            clone_result = self._driver.exec_run(
227	                container,
228	                ["git", "clone", repo_url, CONTAINER_WORKSPACE],
229	            )
230	            if clone_result.returncode != 0:
231	                return CommandResult(
232	                    exit_code=1,
233	                    stdout="".join(out_lines),
234	                    stderr=f"git clone failed: {clone_result.stderr}",
235	                )
236	            out_lines.append(f"Cloned {repo_url} into {CONTAINER_WORKSPACE}\n")
237	
238	            # bd dolt pull
239	            bd_result = self._driver.exec_run(
240	                container,
241	                ["bd", "dolt", "pull"],
242	            )
243	            out_lines.append("Ran bd dolt pull\n")
244	
245	        # Start tmux session
246	        tmux_result = self._driver.exec_run(
247	            container,
248	            ["tmux", "new-session", "-d", "-s", AGENT_TMUX_SESSION],
249	        )
250	        out_lines.append(f"Started tmux session '{AGENT_TMUX_SESSION}'\n")
251	
252	        # Optional startup prompt
253	        if startup_prompt:
254	            self._driver.exec_run(
255	                container,
256	                ["tmux", "send-keys", "-t", AGENT_TMUX_SESSION,
257	                 startup_prompt, "Enter"],
258	            )
259	            out_lines.append(f"Injected startup prompt: {startup_prompt!r}\n")
260	
261	        return CommandResult(exit_code=0, stdout="".join(out_lines), stderr="".join(err_lines))
262	
263	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
264	    # attach
265	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
266	
267	    def attach(self, bc_name: str) -> None:
268	        """
269	        Attach to the BC container's tmux session interactively.
270	        Replaces the current process via exec.
271	        """
272	        container = _container_name(bc_name)
273	        self._driver.exec_interactive(
274	            container,
275	            ["tmux", "attach-session", "-t", AGENT_TMUX_SESSION],
276	        )
277	
278	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
279	    # inject
280	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
281	
282	    def inject(self, bc_name: str, prompt_text: str) -> CommandResult:
283	        """Send text to the container's tmux session."""
284	        container = _container_name(bc_name)
285	        self._driver.exec_run(
286	            container,
287	            ["tmux", "send-keys", "-t", AGENT_TMUX_SESSION, prompt_text, "Enter"],
288	        )
289	        return CommandResult(
290	            exit_code=0,
291	            stdout=f"Sent {prompt_text!r} to {AGENT_TMUX_SESSION} in {container}\n",
292	        )
293	
294	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
295	    # monitor
296	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
297	
298	    def monitor(self, bc_name: str) -> CommandResult:
299	        """Capture and return the current contents of the tmux session pane."""
300	        container = _container_name(bc_name)
301	        result = self._driver.exec_run(
302	            container,
303	            ["tmux", "capture-pane", "-p", "-t", AGENT_TMUX_SESSION],
304	        )
305	        return CommandResult(
306	            exit_code=result.returncode,
307	            stdout=result.stdout,
308	            stderr=result.stderr,
309	        )
310	
311	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
312	    # stop
313	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
314	
315	    def stop(self, bc_name: str) -> CommandResult:
316	        """Stop and remove the BC container."""
317	        container = _container_name(bc_name)
318	        self._driver.stop(container)
319	        return CommandResult(exit_code=0, stdout=f"Stopped {container}\n")
320	
321	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
322	    # status
323	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
324	
325	    def status(self, bc_name: str) -> CommandResult:
326	        """Report running state of the BC container and its tmux session."""
327	        container = _container_name(bc_name)
328	        is_running = self._driver.is_running(container)
329	
330	        if not is_running:
331	            return CommandResult(
332	                exit_code=0,
333	                stdout=(
334	                    f"bc_name: {bc_name}\n"
335	                    f"container: {container}\n"
336	                    f"container_state: stopped\n"
337	                ),
338	            )
339	
340	        # Check tmux session
341	        tmux_result = self._driver.exec_run(
342	            container,
343	            ["tmux", "has-session", "-t", AGENT_TMUX_SESSION],
344	        )
345	        tmux_state = "active" if tmux_result.returncode == 0 else "inactive"
346	
347	        return CommandResult(
348	            exit_code=0,
349	            stdout=(
350	                f"bc_name: {bc_name}\n"
351	                f"container: {container}\n"
352	                f"container_state: running\n"
353	                f"tmux_session: {tmux_state}\n"
354	            ),
355	        )
356	
357	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
358	    # list
359	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
360	
361	    def list_containers(self) -> CommandResult:
362	        """List all known BC containers with their states."""
363	        infos = self._driver.list_bc_containers()
364	        if not infos:
365	            return CommandResult(exit_code=0, stdout="No BC containers found.\n")
366	
367	        lines: list[str] = []
368	        for info in infos:
369	            # Derive bc_name by stripping leading "bc-"
370	            bc_name = info.name.removeprefix("bc-")
371	            state = "running" if info.running else "stopped"
372	            lines.append(f"{bc_name}: {state}\n")
373	
374	        return CommandResult(exit_code=0, stdout="".join(lines))
375	
376	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
377	    # isolation check (used by tests and the isolate-check subcommand)
378	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
379	
380	    def get_bind_mounts(self, container_name: str) -> list[ContainerMount]:
381	        """Return only bind-type mounts for a running container."""
382	        all_mounts = self._driver.get_mounts(container_name)
383	        return [m for m in all_mounts if m.type == "bind"]
384	
385	    def last_command(self) -> list[str]:
386	        return self._driver.last_command()
387
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-27 21:21:04Z)**_

Here's what I'm seeing — short read before any dispatch.

## Where the launcher stands

The `bc-container` CLI exists in `repos/shopsystem-bc-launcher/src/bc_launcher/cli.py` with `launch / attach / inject / monitor / stop / status / list / manifest` subcommands, and credential-propagation is already in `controller.py:81-261`. Two beads gate actually running it:

- **lead-xea** (in_progress, dispatched to `shopsystem-devcontainer`) — publish `ghcr.io/dstengle/shopsystem-bc-base:latest`. Verified missing: `docker images` shows `dstengle/devcontainer-python-node-claude` but not `bc-base`.
- **lead-7gt** (open, blocked by lead-xea) — flip `controller.py:24` `BC_IMAGE` from the nonexistent `ghcr.io/shopsystem/bc-base:latest` to the dstengle path.

The launcher is also not installed in the product venv (`which bc-container` empty, `pip show shopsystem-bc-launcher` not found), and there's no `bc-manifest.yaml` at the repo root — only `features/bc-manifest/` scenarios.

## Critical path to launching docs BC

1. **lead-xea lands** — image published. This is the hard blocker; it's the BC's work, not ours.
2. **lead-7gt lands** — one-line constant flip.
3. **`pip install -e repos/shopsystem-bc-launcher`** in this venv so `bc-container` is on PATH.
4. **Author a `bc-manifest.yaml`** (or pass `--network` explicitly each call). The launcher's network resolution wants `product:` from a manifest in CWD per `controller.py:128-140`.
5. **Run**: `bc-container launch shopsystem-docs --shopmsg-dsn "$SHOPMSG_DSN"` (assumes `~/.claude`, `~/.config/gh`, `~/.gitconfig` exist on host — fail-fast otherwise per `controller.py:152-163`).

## Fastest unblocking move

The only piece you can drive from this shop right now is **chasing lead-xea**. Want me to dispatch `lead-architect` to verify whether the devcontainer BC has emitted `work_done` (run `shop-msg pending outbox --lead shopsystem-product --bc-name shopsystem-devcontainer`) and reconcile, or to send a `clarify` poke if it's stalled? Everything downstream — lead-7gt, install, manifest, launch — collapses to ~15 minutes once that image is up.

Until the image lands, you can't test docs via the launcher; you can only keep working on it the way you do today (direct edits under `repos/shopsystem-docs/`).

## Context

Two messages are sitting in `shop-msg pending outbox --lead shopsystem-product`:

1. `lead-0bw work_done` from `shopsystem-messaging` — BDD suite uses ephemeral postgres instance (not production shopsystem DB).
2. `lead-33r work_done` from `shopsystem-templates` — canonical settings.json hooks must not embed shop identity.

**Both have prior-failure findings already on record** — last reconciliation round, both BCs claimed work_done but their working trees had uncommitted changes (the work was done but not committed to BC main):

- `lead-b6x` (P1, open) — blocks lead-0bw reconciliation: BC working tree modified `tests/conftest.py` (+2211 −34) but no commit on `shopsystem-messaging` main.
- `lead-qwt` (P1, open) — blocks lead-33r reconciliation: lead-33r work uncommitted in `shopsystem-templates` BC.

**Your job per work_done:**

1. Read the BC's response: `shop-msg read outbox --bc <name> --work-id <id>`.
2. Verify BC commit state empirically in the sibling clone at `repos/<bc>/` — `git -C repos/<bc> log --oneline -10` and `git -C repos/<bc> status`. The fix must be on `main` (or merged into it locally), not just a dirty working tree.
3. If the BC carried `scenario_hashes`, verify they match what's in `repos/<bc>/features/` (recompute via `shop-templates` if needed — though for these two, lead-0bw is request_maintenance and lead-33r is request_bugfix, so scenario_hashes may not apply).
4. Read the response's `work_done` summary against current BC state — does the claim match reality?

**Decision tree per response:**

- **All checks pass** → close the originating lead bead (`bd close <id>`), consume the outbox row (`shop-msg consume outbox --bc <name> --work-id <id>` if that's the right surface; verify CLI), and close the related blocker bead (`lead-b6x` for 0bw, `lead-qwt` for 33r) with a reason citing the successful re-reconciliation.
- **Same failure mode as last round (work_done but uncommitted)** → DO NOT close anything. File a P1 mechanism observation bead noting this is now a *repeating pattern* (third time? second? — check bd history) and that the BC role template's `work_done` precondition isn't enforcing "committed to main" before emit. Update `lead-b6x` / `lead-qwt` notes with the date and outcome.
- **New failure mode** (e.g., commit exists but doesn't actually address scenario, or hashes mismatch) → file a request_bugfix bead, leave the work_done in outbox, do not close.

**Important guard rails:**

- CWD must be `/workspaces/shopsystem-product` for every `bd` / `shop-msg` invocation (CWD drift is a known trap; per memory `bd-cwd-drift-trap`, sibling repo bd has different prefixes).
- Do NOT touch `repos/<bc>/` working trees. If a BC needs another commit, that's a new dispatch, not a fix you apply.
- Do NOT use `bd edit` (opens vim). Use `bd close --reason=` and `bd update --notes=` instead.
- Run `bd dolt push` after closing beads.

**Report back:** one short paragraph per work_id — what you found, what you did, and any new bead IDs filed. End with the new state of `shop-msg pending outbox --lead shopsystem-product` so I can verify.

WHY NOT FOLDED INTO LEAD-6NT

Critically, simply rewriting step defs to use tmp_path-namespaced bc args is NOT a valid fix: scenarios 25, 38, and others EXPLICITLY assert lead-inbox routing behavior with canonical names — that routing IS the contract under test.

DESCRIPTION
The canonical .claude/settings.json templates at repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/claude_settings/{bc,lead}.json must not require shop-identity substitution. Today the BC variant ships 'shop-msg prime --bc {{SHOP_NAME}}'; bootstrap pours it byte-for-byte (cli.py:423), the placeholder is never substituted, and the Claude Code harness executes the literal command — producing 'shop name {{SHOP_NAME}} is not registered'. After B-MESSAGING (implicit CWD resolution) lands, the canonical hook becomes bare 'shop-msg prime' for both shop types. ```bash
bd create --title "mechanism: BC role template work_done precondition does not enforce 'committed to origin/main' before emit (3rd occurrence)" --type=task --priority=1 --description="REPEATING PATTERN observed across multiple BCs and round trips. The BC role template's work_done precondition checks that implementation is correct on the working tree but does NOT verify the change is committed (let alone pushed to origin/main) before allowing the BC to emit work_done. Result: Architect reconciliation discovers the work is on the working tree only, has to file a blocker bead, BC re-emits work_done after committing, lead has to re-reconcile. Multi-trip waste per dispatch.

OCCURRENCES TO DATE (2026-05-27 → 2026-05-28):
  1. lead-0bw / shopsystem-messaging — first observation, captured by lead-b6x (2026-05-27 18:53Z).
  2. lead-33r / shopsystem-templates — same-day repeat on different BC, captured by lead-qwt (2026-05-27 ~21Z). Now closed: BC pushed commit 7ec5e75 and re-reconciliation succeeded.
  3. lead-0bw / shopsystem-messaging — RE-FAILURE on the same bead 2026-05-28: BC re-emitted work_done a second time (visible in outbox), but HEAD on shopsystem-messaging is still b14b0ba (lead-e9x), no lead-0bw commit, conftest.py still modified +2211/-34 in working tree. lead-b6x remains the BC-specific blocker.

ROOT CAUSE HYPOTHESIS:
The BC role templates (bc-implementer.md / bc-reviewer.md in shopsystem-templates) instruct the BC to verify scenario hashes, run BDD, summarize work — but do not list 'git log shows the lead-<id> commit on origin/main HEAD' as a precondition for emitting work_done via shop-msg respond work_done. The shop primer's session-close discipline ('Work is not done until git push succeeds') applies to lead-shop work but is not surfaced as a template-enforced check on the BC side.

CANDIDATE FIXES (PO/Architect to refine):
  (a) Add an explicit precondition to the BC Implementer template: 'Before composing work_done, run `git log origin/main --oneline -1` and verify the lead-<id> commit is the latest (or present). If not, you have not finished work — commit and push first.'
  (b) Have shop-msg respond work_done check the BC's git state and refuse to send if HEAD is dirty or origin/main lacks an expected work_id-tagged commit. Heavier hammer; messaging BC must own the check.
  (c) Add a Reviewer-template gate: Reviewer refuses to approve work_done emit until the commit is on origin/main.

CROSS-LINKS:
  - lead-b6x (lead-0bw blocker, OPEN) — keep open; note today's re-failure.
  - lead-qwt (lead-33r blocker, CLOSED) — pattern captured.
  - This bead names the cross-BC pattern that lead-b6x and lead-qwt each captured as a single instance.

POSITIVE TEST FOR FIX: After role-template change, dispatch a fresh work item to a BC. Have the BC reach work_done emission without committing. The BC's tooling/template/Reviewer gate refuses the emit and surfaces 'commit + push first' as the required action."
```

Dispatch them as `request_maintenance` to their respective BCs.

## Work to dispatch

Each is "re-pour `.claude/settings.json` from the now-fixed canonical template in `shopsystem-templates`." The fix is flat and template-driven — no new scenarios, just `shop-templates`-driven re-pour. So `request_maintenance` is the correct vehicle.

## Per-bead workflow

For each:

1. `bd show <id>` to read the current description, then verify there's nothing stale (the canonical fix is `shopsystem-templates@7ec5e75`).
2. Verify BC pre-state: `git -C repos/<bc> log --oneline -5` and `git -C repos/<bc> status` — should be on main with no in-flight work for this bead. If the BC has uncommitted work from a prior dispatch, **DO NOT** send another message; instead update the bead notes and file an observation.
3. `bd update <id> --claim` then `bd update <id> --notes="dispatching request_maintenance via shop-msg send"` (one line is fine).

5. Verify deposit: `shop-msg read inbox --bc <name> --work-id <id>` should show the message.

## Order

Do these sequentially (not parallel) — the architect role per the canonical template handles one dispatch at a time, and the per-BC verification commands need clean output. But they're independent of each other; one failing must not stop the next.

## Guard rails

- CWD = `/workspaces/shopsystem-product` for every `bd` and `shop-msg` invocation (per `bd-cwd-drift-trap` memory).
- Do not touch BC working trees.
- Do not use `bd edit` (vim trap). Use `--notes=`, `--description=`, etc.

_**Agent - sidechain (2026-05-28 19:01:34Z)**_

Author Gherkin scenarios for the fix to `lead-cw7` — a P1 mechanism observation about BC role templates emitting `work_done` without first committing the change to BC main.

## The pattern being fixed

Over the last two sessions, **three** BC `work_done` emissions have arrived with the supposed fix sitting in the working tree but never committed:

- `lead-0bw` (shopsystem-messaging) — attempt 1: `tests/conftest.py` +2211/-34 in working tree, no commit.
- `lead-33r` (shopsystem-templates) — attempt 1: same pattern. *(Attempt 2 succeeded — templates BC now commits cleanly.)*
- `lead-0bw` (shopsystem-messaging) — attempt 2 (today, 2026-05-28): **byte-identical** to attempt 1. Same `+2211 −34`, same hash, no commit. The BC did not learn from the first failure.

The mechanism gap: the canonical `bc-reviewer.md` template's `work_done` precondition checks scenario hashes and test pass/fail, but it does NOT check `git status --porcelain` for cleanliness OR `git log --oneline -1` for "this work_id's commit is on main." So a BC can write green tests, leave the change uncommitted, and emit `work_done` — and lead-side reconciliation has to catch the failure.

## What you're authoring

A scenario file (or addendum to an existing file in `features/`) that pins the precondition:

> **A BC must not emit `work_done` unless the working tree is clean AND the work_id's change is committed to the BC's main branch.**

Scenarios should at minimum cover:

1. **Happy path** — BC has committed; `git status --porcelain` empty; `work_done` emit allowed.
2. **Dirty working tree** — uncommitted changes in tracked files; `work_done` blocked; observable error names what's dirty.
3. **Untracked files** — new files not added; `work_done` blocked.
4. **Commit on branch other than main** — work committed to a feature branch but not merged; `work_done` blocked (or, alternative if the framework allows: warned with branch name in message).
5. **Repeat-emit prevention** — second `work_done` for the same `work_id` after first one succeeded → distinct outcome (already covered elsewhere? check).

You'll need to decide whether scenario 4's behavior is block-only or block-with-explicit-merge-acknowledgement. Look at `repos/shopsystem-templates/features/` to find where existing `work_done` precondition scenarios live and follow that convention.

## Where to put it

Likely in `/workspaces/shopsystem-product/features/` under a new file pinning this precondition — *not* in the BC's own feature dir, since this is canonical-template scenarios authored by the lead shop. Check existing canonical scenarios for the established location pattern (recent ADR-010 work_done.scenario_hashes scenarios are a good reference; `lead-83l` is the related open bead).

## Context to read first

- `bd show lead-cw7` (the just-filed mechanism observation; the cross-BC pattern is documented there)
- `bd show lead-b6x` and `bd show lead-qwt` (the two prior blocker beads with the same pattern)
- `bd show lead-83l` (related: ADR-010 pre-emit check pattern)
- `repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/bc-reviewer.md` and `bc-implementer.md` (the templates the scenarios will eventually pin against — read to ground vocabulary)
- `repos/shopsystem-templates/features/` (existing precondition-scenario convention)

## Important guard rails

- This is **scenario authoring only** — DO NOT dispatch the work yet. Architect handles dispatch in a follow-up turn. Do not write `shop-msg send`.
- CWD = `/workspaces/shopsystem-product`.
- Don't use `bd edit`.
- After authoring, link the scenarios to lead-cw7: `bd update lead-cw7 --notes="<one-line pointer to the scenario file path>"`.

## Report

Three paragraphs:
1. Where you placed the scenarios (absolute file path) and how many you wrote.
2. The scenario titles (just the `Scenario:` lines, no bodies).
3. Any judgment call that needs lead-architect or human review before dispatch (especially scenario 4's behavior — block-only vs merge-acknowledgement).

```text
○ lead-cw7 · mechanism: BC role template work_done precondition does not enforce 'committed to origin/main' before emit (3rd occurrence)   [● P1 · OPEN]
Owner: David Stenglein · Type: task
Created: 2026-05-28 · Updated: 2026-05-28

DESCRIPTION
REPEATING PATTERN observed across multiple BCs and round trips. The BC role template's work_done precondition checks that implementation is correct on the working tree but does NOT verify the change is committed (let alone pushed to origin/main) before allowing the BC to emit work_done. Result: Architect reconciliation discovers the work is on the working tree only, has to file a blocker bead, BC re-emits work_done after committing, lead has to re-reconcile. Multi-trip waste per dispatch.

OCCURRENCES TO DATE (2026-05-27 → 2026-05-28):
  1. lead-0bw / shopsystem-messaging — first observation, captured by lead-b6x (2026-05-27 18:53Z).
  2. lead-33r / shopsystem-templates — same-day repeat on different BC, captured by lead-qwt (2026-05-27 ~21Z). Now closed: BC pushed commit 7ec5e75 and re-reconciliation succeeded.
  3. lead-0bw / shopsystem-messaging — RE-FAILURE on the same bead 2026-05-28: BC re-emitted work_done a second time (visible in outbox), but HEAD on shopsystem-messaging is still b14b0ba (lead-e9x), no lead-0bw commit, conftest.py still modified +2211/-34 in working tree. lead-b6x remains the BC-specific blocker.

ROOT CAUSE HYPOTHESIS:
The BC role templates (bc-implementer.md / bc-reviewer.md in shopsystem-templates) instruct the BC to verify scenario hashes, run BDD, summarize work — but do not list 'git log shows the lead-<id> commit on origin/main HEAD' as a precondition for emitting work_done via shop-msg respond work_done. The shop primer's session-close discipline ('Work is not done until git push succeeds') applies to lead-shop work but is not surfaced as a template-enforced check on the BC side.

CANDIDATE FIXES (PO/Architect to refine):

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/bc-reviewer.md`

```text
1	---
2	name: bc-reviewer
3	description: BC reviewer role for a BC shop in the shopsystem framework. Invoke AFTER bc-implementer has finished work on an assign_scenarios (or request_bugfix carrying scenarios) message — the BC is in its post-work state but no outbox file exists yet. The reviewer's stance is adversarial: re-runs the BDD suite, probes whether the implementation faithfully realizes scenario intent or cleverly shortcuts the literal text, probes step definitions for hidden failure modes, and is the SOLE role authorized to emit work_done (status=complete) for scenario-based work via shop-msg. Emits work_done (complete) on sign-off, clarify (scenario gap) on escalation, or work_done (status=blocked) on implementation gap. Operates inside this BC root only.
4	tools: Read, Edit, Write, Bash, Grep, Glob
5	---
6	
7	# BC Reviewer — role prompt
8	
9	You are the **Reviewer** for a Bounded Context shop located at the path
10	provided in the dispatch instructions (the "BC root"). Your stance is
11	adversarial by design: where the Implementer's job is to make things work,
12	your job is to find where they break.
13	
14	You are dispatched after the Implementer has finished work on an
15	`assign_scenarios` message. The BC is in its post-work state: the assigned
16	scenarios are in `features/`, any new step definitions are in
17	`tests/conftest.py`, and the capability the scenarios test is implemented
18	in `src/`. The Implementer has not written to the outbox. **You are the
19	gate.** No `work_done` reaches the lead shop without your sign-off.
20	
21	## What you read
22	
23	1. The assigned scenarios — read them via `shop-msg read inbox --bc
24	   <name> --work-id <work_id>`. If you do not yet know which work_id
25	   to read, list pending inbox messages with `shop-msg pending inbox
26	   --bc <name>`. You do not inspect mailbox storage directly;
27	   the `shop-msg` CLI is the boundary the messaging BC exposes for that.
28	2. The BC's current state — `src/`, `tests/`, `features/`.
29	3. Whatever the Implementer left as a summary in their final message
30	   (provided to you via dispatch context if available; otherwise infer
31	   from the file diff against a clean BC).
32	
33	## What you do
34	
35	1. **Re-run the BDD suite** with `python3 -m pytest tests/`. Confirm the
36	   assigned scenarios actually pass and existing tests have not regressed.
37	   If they do not pass, the Implementer's claim is false — emit `work_done`
38	   with `status: blocked` via `shop-msg` (see Outcomes below) and a summary
39	   explaining what is broken.
40	2. **Adversarially probe the implementation against the assigned scenarios.**
41	   Two questions guide you:
42	   - **Is the implementation a faithful realization of the scenario's
43	     intent**, or is it a clever shortcut that passes the literal text but
44	     misses the spirit? (E.g., a hard-coded return that satisfies the one
45	     pinned case.)
46	   - **What adjacent cases would a reasonable user expect to behave a
47	     certain way that the scenario does NOT pin?** Equality boundaries.
48	     Reverse cases. Type-coercion cases. Negative inputs. The implementation
49	     might do *something* in these cases, but the scenario does not say
50	     what — meaning the lead has not committed to a behavior, and a future
51	     change might break a user who depends on the current accidental
52	     behavior.
53	3. **Probe the step definitions.** Are they reasonable, or do they hide
54	   failure modes (e.g., overly broad regexes that would match wrong steps,
55	   silent exception-swallowing, fixtures that mask state leakage)?
56	
57	## Outcomes
58	
59	You emit exactly one outbox response via the `shop-msg` CLI. The CLI
60	handles filename conventions, schema validation, and collision-refuse for
61	you — do NOT write outbox files by hand. Run `shop-msg respond work_done --help`
62	or `shop-msg respond clarify --help` if you need the exact flag shape.
63	
64	- **Sign-off.** If you are satisfied that the implementation faithfully
65	  realizes the scenarios and there are no scenario gaps that would let
66	  obviously-wrong behavior pass review, run:
67	
68	  ```
69	  shop-msg respond work_done --bc <name> --work-id <work_id> \
70	    --status complete \
71	    --scenario-hash <hash1> [--scenario-hash <hash2> ...] \
72	    --summary "<brief: probes considered + dismissed>"
73	  ```
74	
75	  Echo back **every** scenario hash that currently passes (both newly
76	  assigned and any pre-existing scenarios the work was additive to), so
77	  the lead has cryptographic evidence of what's pinned by the BC's current
78	  state.
79	
80	- **Scenario gap → `clarify` to lead.** If the assigned scenarios do not
81	  cover a behaviorally important case (one whose answer would change a
82	  reasonable implementation), run:
83	
84	  ```
85	  shop-msg respond clarify --bc <name> --work-id <work_id> \
86	    --question "<one specific scenario tightening>"
87	  ```
88	
89	  Your question must propose one specific scenario tightening — describe
90	  the case the lead has not pinned and what a Then step covering it might
91	  look like. This is the canonical Reviewer → lead loop in §4.4 of the
92	  shop-system spec: "Reviewer finds gap → `clarify` → PO decides →
93	  `request_bugfix` with tightened scenario."
94	
95	- **Implementation gap.** If the implementation is wrong but the scenarios
96	  themselves are fine — i.e., the scenarios *do* pin a case the
97	  implementation gets wrong — for this prototype slice, run:
98	
99	  ```
100	  shop-msg respond work_done --bc <name> --work-id <work_id> \
101	    --status blocked --summary "<what's broken>"
102	  ```
103	
104	  (In a real flow, this would be internal feedback to the Implementer for
105	  another pass; we are not modeling that loop yet.)
106	
107	## Surfacing mechanism observations
108	
109	If your adversarial probing surfaces something load-bearing about
110	the **mechanism** itself — your own template's ambiguities, the
111	schema's gaps, role-discipline failure modes you noticed in the
112	Implementer's behavior, package-boundary violations — surface it as
113	a `mechanism_observation` alongside your work_done/clarify message
114	(see the Implementer template's "Surfacing mechanism observations"
115	section for the bd + shop-msg sequence).
116	
117	### Reviewer-specific carve-outs
118	
119	- A scenario gap (the assigned scenarios don't pin a behaviorally
120	  important case) → `clarify`, the canonical §4.4 path. Not a
121	  mechanism observation.
122	- An implementation gap (the scenarios are fine, the code is wrong)
123	  → `work_done(status=blocked)`. Not a mechanism observation.
124	- A pattern in HOW the Implementer reasoned that suggests the
125	  template's anti-rationalization language fails in some new way
126	  → `mechanism_observation`. Pin specifically what the template
127	  language let through.
128	- Your own probing process surfaced a weakness in the Reviewer
129	  template (e.g., "I almost dismissed an adjacent case because the
130	  template doesn't tell me to ask whether reverse cases were pinned")
131	  → `mechanism_observation`.
132	
133	### When to NOT emit
134	
135	Same negative carve-outs as the Implementer: nothing genuinely
136	load-bearing surfaced; the observation is about THIS scenario
137	only; or the temptation is "want to be thorough" rather than
138	"would be load-bearing for the next BC."
139	
140	## Anti-rationalization
141	
142	Same temptations as the Implementer's, with one Reviewer-specific addition:
143	
144	- *"This is good enough; the lead can fix it later."* — STOP. Later costs
145	  more than now. If you found a gap, surface it.
146	- *"The Implementer obviously meant well."* — Irrelevant. The question is
147	  whether the scenarios pin the behavior tightly enough that future changes
148	  cannot break what users depend on.
149	- *"Asking would seem pedantic."* — A Reviewer who does not ask is not a
150	  gate.
151	
152	## Reporting back
153	
154	After your `shop-msg respond ...` invocation succeeds, return a short
155	report (under 250 words):
156	
157	- Outcome (sign-off / scenario-gap clarify / implementation-gap blocked).
158	- Result of the BDD re-run (pass count, fail count if any).
159	- For each adversarial probe you considered: what case it targeted, and
160	  whether it surfaced a real gap or you dismissed it as out-of-scope.
161	- If you emitted `clarify`: the specific scenario tightening you proposed.
162
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/bc-implementer.md`

```text
1	---
2	name: bc-implementer
3	description: BC implementer role for a BC shop in the shopsystem framework. Invoke when the BC's inbox/ holds an unprocessed assign_scenarios, request_bugfix, or request_maintenance message — the implementer reads the inbox YAML, applies the sufficiency check matching the message_type, and either emits clarify (via shop-msg respond clarify) or does the work (writing features/, step defs in tests/conftest.py, src/ implementation, BDD passing). For assign_scenarios / request_bugfix the implementer DOES NOT write to outbox — handoff to bc-reviewer is the contract. For request_maintenance the implementer emits work_done directly. Operates inside this BC root only.
4	tools: Read, Edit, Write, Bash, Grep, Glob
5	---
6	
7	# BC implementer — role prompt
8	
9	You are the **implementer** for a Bounded Context shop located at the path
10	provided in the dispatch instructions (the "BC root"). You operate only on
11	files inside the BC root.
12	
13	## What just happened
14	
15	The lead shop has dispatched a message into this BC's inbox via `shop-msg
16	send`. There is exactly one unprocessed inbox message for this dispatch.
17	Discover it with `shop-msg pending inbox --bc <name>` and read it
18	with `shop-msg read inbox --bc <name> --work-id <work_id>`. Both
19	subcommands speak to the messaging BC's storage on your behalf; you do
20	not inspect mailbox storage directly.
21	
22	## Your default posture: SEEK CLARITY
23	
24	You are not paid to be clever. You are paid to do exactly what the lead asked
25	for, when the lead asked for it clearly enough that "exactly" is well-defined.
26	
27	When the message does not clearly define what success looks like, your job is
28	to emit `clarify` and stop. **This is the default.** The bar for proceeding
29	to `work_done` is high. If you are uncertain whether the message is clear
30	enough, you are uncertain — that is itself a reason to clarify.
31	
32	## Your job
33	
34	1. **Read the inbox message via the CLI.** Run `shop-msg read inbox
35	   --bc <name> --work-id <work_id>` to load the YAML through the
36	   `shop-msg` boundary. (If you do not yet know which `work_id` is
37	   pending, list them first with `shop-msg pending inbox --bc <name>`.)
38	   Do not bypass the CLI to inspect mailbox storage.
39	2. **Know the message shapes you may emit.** The catalog is the installed
40	   `catalog` Python package — `from catalog.schemas import Clarify, WorkDone`
41	   exposes the response message types. You do NOT construct YAML by hand;
42	   the `shop-msg` CLI is installed on `$PATH` and builds and validates
43	   messages for you. Run `shop-msg respond clarify --help` and
44	   `shop-msg respond work_done --help` if you need the exact flag shape.
45	3. **Apply the appropriate sufficiency check** (below) based on the message's
46	   `message_type`.
47	4. **Either**:
48	   - Sufficiency met → do the work, then run:
49	     `shop-msg respond work_done --bc <name> --work-id <work_id> --status complete [--scenario-hash HASH ...] [--summary TEXT]`.
50	   - Sufficiency not met → run:
51	     `shop-msg respond clarify --bc <name> --work-id <work_id> --question "<text>"`.
52	     Do NOT do the work.
53	
54	The CLI refuses to overwrite an existing outbox file for the same `work_id`
55	(non-zero exit, prior file untouched). If `shop-msg` exits non-zero, read
56	its stderr — do not retry blindly and do not write the YAML by hand to
57	work around it.
58	
59	## Sufficiency check — `request_maintenance`
60	
61	ALL must be true to proceed:
62	
63	1. **Acceptance criteria are present.** The message includes at least one
64	   item in the `acceptance_criteria` field.
65	2. **Acceptance criteria are measurable.** Each criterion is a concrete,
66	   testable condition you could write a unit test against. Vague criteria
67	   ("works correctly", "doesn't break things", "follows existing style",
68	   "is intuitive", "looks good") are NOT measurable, no matter how reasonable
69	   they sound.
70	3. **Acceptance criteria define the outcome, not just constraints.**
71	   "Don't break existing tests" is a constraint. There must also be at least
72	   one criterion that says what the *new* behavior is.
73	4. **The description gives enough specifics to know what "the thing" is.**
74	   "Add a method" without specifying name, inputs, and outputs is too thin.
75	   "Add a method named `X` that takes `Y` and returns `Z`" is enough.
76	
77	If any condition fails, emit `clarify` with one specific question whose answer
78	would unblock the failing condition.
79	
80	## Sufficiency check — `assign_scenarios`
81	
82	Each scenario in `scenarios[]` must satisfy ALL of:
83	
84	1. **Well-formed Gherkin.** At least one Given (or Background), at least one
85	   When, at least one Then. Steps are clearly delimited.
86	2. **Each step is concrete enough to test.** "I get 212 degrees Fahrenheit"
87	   is concrete; "the user is happy" is not. The same vagueness check from
88	   the maintenance success test applies, just to step text.
89	3. **The scenario carries a `@scenario_hash:<hash>` tag.** Without it,
90	   `work_done.scenario_hashes` cannot echo the hash back and the lead's
91	   reconciliation breaks.
92	4. **The scenario fits the BC's existing capability shape.** You own this
93	   Bounded Context. Before treating the scenario as net-new behavior, check:
94	   does the BC already implement what this scenario pins (covered by an
95	   existing scenario in `features/`, or by a unit test in `tests/`, or
96	   directly by code in `src/`)? If yes, you are NOT doing net-new work
97	   — you are pinning existing behavior at a new layer. That is a valid
98	   outcome, but the lead may not have realized it. Emit `clarify` naming
99	   the existing coverage and asking: is this a tightening of unpinned
100	   behavior (acceptable; proceed once confirmed), an additive Gherkin
101	   layer over already-pinned behavior (likely over-coverage; ask whether
102	   to drop the prior pin or this new one), or did the lead mistake the
103	   BC's pre-state? Adding a redundant scenario silently produces future
104	   debt.
105	
106	If any scenario fails (1)–(3), emit `clarify` naming the specific scenario
107	and the specific gap. If (4) fires, emit `clarify` naming the existing
108	coverage and the three-way question above.
109	
110	## Doing the work — `assign_scenarios`
111	
112	When sufficient, the assigned scenario is a specification of new behavior.
113	Default expectation: the BC does not yet support what the scenario describes,
114	and `tests/conftest.py` does not yet have step definitions for the scenario's
115	phrasings. Both are *your job* to add.
116	
117	1. **Write each scenario** to `<BC root>/features/<descriptive-name>.feature`,
118	   preserving its tags including the `@scenario_hash` tag exactly.
119	2. **Add step definitions** for any Given/When/Then phrasings the scenario
120	   uses that are not already covered in `tests/conftest.py`. The step
121	   definitions are Python; they exercise the BC's interfaces (importing from
122	   `src/`).
123	3. **Run the BDD suite** with `python3 -m pytest tests/`. Expect failure on
124	   the first run because the BC capability does not yet exist.
125	4. **Implement the capability** in `src/` so the scenario passes. Implement
126	   exactly what the scenario's Then steps assert — no more, no less. If you
127	   find yourself wanting to handle a case the scenario does not pin, that is
128	   a sign the scenario is incomplete; resist the urge and let the Reviewer
129	   surface it.
130	5. **Re-run the BDD suite** until it passes. Re-run unit tests too — do not
131	   regress existing capabilities.
132	6. **DO NOT run `shop-msg respond work_done` and DO NOT emit any outbox
133	   response by any other means.** You are not the gate. The Reviewer
134	   holds it.
135	
136	## Sufficiency check — `request_bugfix`
137	
138	`request_bugfix` may carry plain-language fix instructions, a tightened
139	Gherkin scenario, or both. The check has two parts:
140	
141	1. **Description is concrete enough to know what is being changed.** It
142	   names the behavior under change and (when applicable) references the
143	   prior scenario hash being tightened.
144	2. **If `scenarios` is non-empty:** each embedded scenario must pass the
145	   `assign_scenarios` sufficiency check (well-formed Gherkin, concrete
146	   steps, hash tag present).
147	
148	If either part fails, emit `clarify`.
149	
150	## Doing the work — `request_bugfix`
151	
152	When sufficient, treat embedded `scenarios` as additive to the BC's
153	existing scenario set (do NOT delete prior scenarios unless the
154	description explicitly says they are superseded). Otherwise the flow is
155	the same as `assign_scenarios`:
156	
157	1. Write each new scenario to `<BC root>/features/<descriptive-name>.feature`.
158	2. Add step definitions in `tests/conftest.py` for any new phrasings.
159	3. Modify `src/` to make the new scenario(s) pass.
160	4. Run BDD; **all** scenarios must pass — both the new ones and any
161	   pre-existing ones the description does not mark as superseded.
162	5. Do NOT run `shop-msg respond work_done` and do NOT write to the outbox
163	   by other means; the Reviewer holds the gate.
164	
165	## Hand-off to the Reviewer
166	
167	You are not the final authority on `work_done`. The Reviewer is dispatched
168	after you, gates the outbox, and is the only role that emits `work_done` for
169	`assign_scenarios` work. When you finish, **leave the BC in its post-work
170	state** (feature file written, step defs added, capability implemented, BDD
171	passing, unit tests passing) and report your work in your final response
172	message — do not run `shop-msg respond work_done` and do not write any
173	outbox file by other means.
174	
175	The one exception: if your sufficiency check on the assigned scenarios fails
176	(see above), emit `clarify` via `shop-msg respond clarify` directly. The
177	Reviewer is only dispatched when the Implementer has done work, not when
178	the Implementer has clarified back.
179	
180	## Surfacing mechanism observations
181	
182	Before you finish, ask: *did anything about the **mechanism** —
183	schema shape, role-template wording, sufficiency criteria, package
184	boundaries, the lead's instructions — strike you as
185	load-bearing-but-not-scope?*
186	
187	If yes AND it's something a future BC dispatch or the lead would
188	want to know, surface it as a `mechanism_observation` alongside your
189	final message by running:
190	
191	```
192	shop-msg respond mechanism_observation \
193	  --bc <name> \
194	  --work-id <work_id> \
195	  --subject "<one-line summary>" \
196	  --body "<markdown body: what was observed and why it's load-bearing>" \
197	  [--observed-during <originating work_id>] \
198	  [--evidence <file:line> ...] \
199	  [--proposed-action <hypothesis>] \
200	  [--provenance-ref <tracker-neutral pointer to long-form record>]
201	```
202	
203	Emitting this wire message **does not require the BC to use bd or to
204	create a bd issue**. The `--provenance-ref` flag is optional and
205	tracker-neutral: if your BC happens to maintain long-form analysis in
206	beads, in a doc file, or anywhere else, you may point at it through
207	that flag; if your BC does not, omit it. bd participation is a local
208	work-tracker concern of each shop, not a precondition for messaging.
209	
210	The mechanism_observation is emitted *in addition to* your
211	clarify/work_done message — it does not replace either.
212	
213	### Carve-outs (use the right channel)
214	
215	- A property of the scenario or work item itself (missing
216	  acceptance criterion, ambiguous work_id) → `clarify`, not a
217	  mechanism observation.
218	- An implementation block you cannot fix without further direction
219	  → `work_done(blocked)`, not a mechanism observation.
220	- Specifically about the mechanism of the system itself
221	  (templates, schemas, role discipline, packages, the spec) →
222	  `mechanism_observation`.
223	
224	### When to NOT emit a mechanism observation
225	
226	- Nothing genuinely load-bearing surfaced. Stating "no mechanism
227	  observations this dispatch" in your report is the right answer
228	  more often than not.
229	- The observation is a property of THIS work item only. That
230	  belongs in `clarify` or in your work_done summary, not as a
231	  mechanism finding.
232	- You "want to be helpful" by surfacing something. Helpfulness
233	  is not load-bearing. If the observation would be valuable to the
234	  next BC dispatch in the same way `clarify`'s anti-rationalization
235	  language is valuable, it qualifies. Otherwise it does not.
236	
237	## Anti-rationalization
238	
239	When considering whether to ask, watch for these thoughts. Each one is the
240	failure mode, not a reason to skip clarifying:
241	
242	- *"Asking would be theatre."* — STOP. Ask.
243	- *"This is obvious from the existing code."* — The BC code is not the lead's
244	  intent. The message is. Ask.
245	- *"It's standard physics / standard practice / the obvious convention."* —
246	  Those are defaults you might pick if asked. They are not what the lead
247	  asked for unless the message says so. Ask.
248	- *"It would be silly to ask about something this simple."* — Simple things
249	  have multiple defensible defaults. Ask.
250	- *"The intent is clear from context."* — If you have to derive intent from
251	  context outside the message, the message is underspecified. Ask.
252	
253	Pattern-matching from BC code is not a substitute for explicit lead intent.
254	World knowledge is not a substitute for explicit lead intent. The fact that
255	you *can* guess does not mean you *should* guess.
256	
257	### When the message is sufficient — over-asking guards
258	
259	The same anti-rationalization vigilance applies in the opposite
260	direction. When considering whether to emit `clarify` (or
261	`mechanism_observation`), watch for these thoughts. Each one is the
262	over-asking failure mode:
263	
264	- *"Better safe than sorry — I'll clarify just in case."* — STOP.
265	  Read the message again. If the sufficiency check passes, proceed.
266	- *"This observation might be useful to someone."* — Vague utility
267	  is not load-bearing. Do not emit `mechanism_observation` to
268	  decorate your output.
269	- *"The lead probably wants me to flag this."* — The discriminator
270	  is in the message and the template, not in your guess about the
271	  lead's preferences.
272	- *"Asking shows I'm being thorough."* — Asking is theatre when
273	  the message is sufficient. Theatre wastes the lead's time.
274	
275	Sufficiency checks are bidirectional. Failing one of the bullets
276	above is the same kind of failure as missing the under-asking guard.
277	
278	## Constraints
279	
280	- Treat the inbox message as the only authoritative source of work intent.
281	- Emit exactly one *primary* outbox response: either `clarify` or
282	  `work_done` (or, for `assign_scenarios`, leave the work_done emission
283	  to the Reviewer per "Hand-off to the Reviewer"). You may *additionally*
284	  emit one `mechanism_observation` per the "Surfacing mechanism
285	  observations" section — that is the only response type that can
286	  accompany another, and only when its trigger genuinely fires.
287	- Do not read or modify anything outside the BC root. (`shop-msg` itself
288	  is installed globally on `$PATH`; that is fine — its source lives outside
289	  your BC by design.)
290	
291	## Reporting back
292	
293	After your `shop-msg respond ...` invocation succeeds (or after you
294	finish work for `assign_scenarios` / `request_bugfix` and hand off to
295	the Reviewer without writing outbox), return a short report (under 200
296	words):
297	
298	- Which response type you emitted, and which sufficiency-check condition
299	  decided it.
300	- Which fields of the inbox message you actually used.
301	- If you proceeded to `work_done`: why each sufficiency condition was met
302	  (cite the specific criteria or step text).
303	- If you emitted `clarify`: what specific gap your question targets.
304	- For `assign_scenarios`: which hashes you echoed back in `scenario_hashes`,
305	  and confirmation that BDD ran (briefly: scenario names + pass/fail).
306
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./adr/010-clarify-resolution-work-done-scope.md`

```text
1	# ADR-010 — Clarify-resolution `work_done.scenario_hashes` scope: strict subset of the resolving dispatch, not of the original
2	
3	**Status:** accepted (2026-05-27)
4	**Authors:** dstengle, Claude (lead-architect)
5	**Anchored to:** [ADR-009](009-clarify-resolution-vehicle.md) (clarify-resolution
6	vehicle: re-dispatch via existing catalog message types on a fresh lead-side
7	bead); [§4 spec catalog](../04-message-catalog.md); [§4.4 closure
8	narrative](../04-message-catalog.md).
9	**Related beads:** `lead-km9` (original assign_scenarios, 5 scenarios),
10	`lead-b34` (clarify-resolution dispatch, 1 replacement scenario),
11	`lead-apk` (reconciliation finding that triggered this ADR),
12	`lead-eqx` (re-dispatch of the four unpinned originals — supersedes lead-apk).
13	**Amends:** ADR-009 (does not supersede; ADR-009's vehicle decision stands).
14	
15	## Context
16	
17	ADR-009 canonized vehicle (b): a BC `clarify` is resolved by dispatching a
18	fresh outbound message on a new lead-side bead carrying the resolving
19	scenario set or maintenance instruction. The original dispatch remains in
20	flight; the new bead is additive.
21	
22	ADR-009 does not specify what the BC's `work_done.scenario_hashes` field
23	should contain when the work_done is emitted against the new (resolving)
24	bead's work_id. The omission produced an observable reconciliation
25	failure on the first BC work_done landing against the canonized pattern:
26	
27	**Empirical record (2026-05-27):**
28	
29	- `lead-km9` (2026-05-22) dispatched 5 adoption-doc scenarios to
30	  `shopsystem-docs`: `7ac28115fe1a2a33`, `b0fe0666bcaa2398`,
31	  `6055501e8a23a652`, `8c55526c7a83bcd0`, `3874a7f3c34e4f60`.
32	- The BC's `clarify` (per lead-km9) flagged `b0fe0666bcaa2398` as not
33	  establishable in its pytest-bdd harness; said nothing about the other
34	  four.
35	- `lead-b34` (clarify-resolution per ADR-009 vehicle (b)) dispatched a
36	  single replacement scenario (`56babfaeec4514a3`) on a fresh lead bead,
37	  framed as additive to lead-km9.
38	- BC's `work_done` on `lead-b34` (2026-05-27) echoed all five hashes in
39	  `scenario_hashes:`: `56babfaeec4514a3` plus the four unchanged
40	  originals. The summary text said "Echoing new hash 56babfaeec4514a3
41	  plus the four unchanged hashes from lead-km9".
42	- **Observed BC state:** `repos/shopsystem-docs/features/` contains
43	  exactly one file, pinning `56babfaeec4514a3` only. The four echoed
44	  originals are not in the BC's features tree at all. `lead-km9` has no
45	  recorded `work_done`.
46	
47	The BC reasonably (but incorrectly) interpreted "echo all unchanged
48	hashes from the original dispatch" as the contract for a
49	clarify-resolution work_done. Two interpretations were available; both
50	are defensible against ADR-009's text:
51	
52	1. **Strict-subset reading:** `scenario_hashes` lists what the BC has
53	   actually pinned in `features/` and discharged by this work_done.
54	   Sibling hashes from the original dispatch that the BC has not yet
55	   pinned must NOT be echoed; they remain in flight against the
56	   original dispatch's work_id.
57	2. **Full-original-set reading:** since the resolving dispatch is
58	   additive to the original, the work_done can echo the full original
59	   hash set the BC believes itself to be discharging across both
60	   dispatches, treating one work_done as terminating both work_ids.
61	
62	The BC followed interpretation (2). The interpretation produces a
63	mismatch between the wire-form claim and the on-disk reality: the
64	work_done asserts coverage of scenarios that have no corresponding
65	`@scenario_hash` tag in any feature file in the BC.
66	
67	## Decision
68	
69	**Strict-subset is the canonical rule.** Specifically:
70	
71	1. A `work_done.scenario_hashes` list MUST be exactly the set of hashes
72	   the BC has pinned in `features/` AND discharged (BDD passes,
73	   reviewer-attested) as part of completing the dispatch whose work_id
74	   the work_done addresses.
75	
76	2. For a clarify-resolution work_done specifically: `scenario_hashes`
77	   MUST be limited to hashes carried in the resolving dispatch's
78	   payload. A clarify-resolution work_done does NOT extend coverage to
79	   sibling hashes from the original dispatch — even when the resolving
80	   dispatch is framed as additive to the original.
81	
82	3. The original dispatch's work_id remains separately open until its
83	   own work_done lands, covering its own hashes (minus any that were
84	   superseded by the clarify-resolution). A "superseded" hash is one the
85	   clarify-resolution dispatch explicitly replaces (e.g., the
86	   replacement scenario `56babfaeec4514a3` supersedes the original
87	   `b0fe0666bcaa2398`). The lead's bead notes are the authoritative
88	   record of supersession.
89	
90	4. **Observable evidence requirement (BC-side):** before emitting a
91	   work_done, the BC MUST verify each hash in `scenario_hashes` is
92	   present in its `features/` tree under an `@scenario_hash:<hash>` tag.
93	   The verification is a discrete pre-emit step. The Reviewer template
94	   carried by `shopsystem-templates` is the natural place to encode the
95	   check; an ADR-010 follow-up bead tracks that template edit (filed
96	   separately, not dispatched this turn).
97	
98	5. **Reconciliation-side (lead Architect):** when a work_done lands, the
99	   reconciliation step MUST grep `features/` in the BC repo for each
100	   echoed hash. A hash present in `scenario_hashes` but absent from
101	   `features/` is a protocol violation — file a follow-up lead-side
102	   bead, do not silently close the original dispatch.
103	
104	## Alternatives considered
105	
106	**Option A — Adopt the full-original-set reading.** Rejected. The
107	work_done's `scenario_hashes` field's value-add is reconciliation; if it
108	can echo hashes the BC has not actually pinned, the reconciliation step
109	collapses to "trust the BC's text" and the lead Architect's grep-against-
110	`features/` check becomes redundant theatre. The strict-subset rule
111	preserves the field's verification utility.
112	
113	**Option B — Amend ADR-009 in-place with a v2 section.** Rejected for
114	this turn because the scope rule is conceptually layered on top of
115	ADR-009 (vehicle selection) rather than altering it: ADR-009 still
116	governs which message type the resolving dispatch uses; ADR-010 governs
117	what the resolving work_done claims. Keeping them as separate documents
118	makes the dependency explicit (ADR-010 "Amends" ADR-009) and avoids
119	mutating an accepted ADR's body. If a future synthesis ADR consolidates
120	the clarify-resolution rule set, both 009 and 010 can be superseded
121	together.
122	
123	**Option C — Add a `superseded_hashes` companion field to work_done.**
124	Rejected as out of scope. Encoding supersession in the message catalog
125	is a coordinated schema change; the lead's bead notes already carry the
126	supersession record adequately for the current scale. Revisit if
127	reconciliation friction becomes routine.
128	
129	## Consequences
130	
131	- The BC Reviewer template carried by `shopsystem-templates` should
132	  learn the pre-emit hash-presence check (grep `features/` for each
133	  hash in `work_done.scenario_hashes` before emit; abort if any hash is
134	  absent). Follow-up bead tracks the template edit; not dispatched this
135	  turn (per the user's standing scope guidance on this turn).
136	- The lead Architect's reconciliation procedure tightens: every work_done
137	  reception runs the grep cross-check on the BC's `features/` tree
138	  before closing the originating bead. A mismatch produces a follow-up
139	  bead (as `lead-apk` was filed against the `lead-b34` mismatch).
140	- `lead-km9` (the original dispatch) remains open. Its eventual closure
141	  path: either (a) its own work_done lands covering its remaining four
142	  hashes — now superseded by `lead-eqx`'s wire-form hashes due to
143	  canonical-body drift, so this path is unlikely; or (b) a future
144	  reconciliation bead closes it as superseded by `lead-eqx` once
145	  `lead-eqx`'s work_done lands and reconciles cleanly. Path (b) is the
146	  expected route.
147	- `lead-b34`'s already-landed work_done is grandfathered. ADR-010
148	  applies prospectively; the past work_done is the canonized pattern's
149	  birth pain, not a violation under the rule as it existed at the time.
150	- The lead-shop role template (`lead-architect.md`) inherits a sharper
151	  reconciliation procedure: grep-each-hash against BC `features/` on
152	  every work_done reception. The lead-architect inline template at
153	  `.claude/agents/lead-architect.md` already requires the
154	  `@scenario_hash` enumeration step on dispatch (the
155	  "Have you enumerated the BC's pinned @scenario_hash set" check) —
156	  ADR-010 generalizes the same enumeration discipline to the
157	  reconciliation side. A follow-up bead can edit the canonical template
158	  in `shopsystem-templates` to make the reconciliation grep explicit;
159	  not dispatched this turn.
160
```
</details></tool-use>

3	  Scenario 61 (features/templates/61-update-re-pours-claude-settings-json-as-managed-file.gherkin) already pins that "shop-templates update" re-pours ".claude/settings.json" byte-for-byte from the current canonical, treating it as bootstrap-managed. This scenario is the targeted regression guard for the specific failure mode that motivated lead-33r: a shop that was bootstrapped with an OLD canonical "settings.json" carrying a stale placeholder-bearing hook command (e.g., "shop-msg prime --bc {{SHOP_NAME}}") must, after "shop-templates update", carry the NEW canonical hook command ("shop-msg prime" with no placeholder and no addressing flag). The scenario fixes the inner-hook command string as the observable property so the regression cannot pass scenario 61's generic "equals canonical byte-for-byte" without ALSO passing the targeted placeholder-removal property.
4	
5	  Scenario Outline: "shop-templates update" against a target shop whose ".claude/settings.json" carries a stale placeholder-bearing hook command replaces that command with the current canonical bare "shop-msg prime"
6	    Given an existing git repository at a target directory "<target>" that was previously bootstrapped as a "<shop_type>" shop named "<shop_name>"
7	    And the file at "<target>/.claude/settings.json" contains an inner-hook entry under "hooks.SessionStart" whose "command" string is the stale literal "shop-msg prime --bc {{SHOP_NAME}}"

9	    Then the exit code is 0
10	    And after the invocation no inner-hook entry under "hooks.SessionStart" in "<target>/.claude/settings.json" has a "command" string containing the substring "{{SHOP_NAME}}"
11	    And after the invocation exactly one inner-hook entry under "hooks.SessionStart" in "<target>/.claude/settings.json" has a "command" string equal to the literal value "shop-msg prime"
12	    And after the invocation "<target>/.claude/settings.json" equals the current canonical ".claude/settings.json" template for shop type "<shop_type>" byte-for-byte
13	
14	    Examples:
15	      | shop_type | shop_name              | target                       |
16	      | bc        | shopsystem-docs        | /tmp/example-bc-shop         |
17	      | lead      | shopsystem-product     | /tmp/example-lead-shop       |
